## [00:00 - 01:33]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Lumer and today we're going to talk about a potential problem with AI chatbots. Let's go. This is what worries me about AI chatbots. When you create a chatbot, you provide the content that it's trained on. The files, the text, or website URLs. You can have it crawl the entire site map of a domain or submit the links individually. It doesn't matter if you own that content. You could then build and market a resource on that topic that is trained entirely on content that you do not own. This is a debate about chat GPT and large language models generally. But this example feels much more clear-cut. There are potential solutions that would fall on the app developer. First, require domain verification before submitting links or site map. Second, create a tool that scans text and documents for matches on the internet. The easiest solution? Require all bots to link to their trained material. You'll be able to see exactly where that content comes from. You can bet that lawsuits will be coming and this type of transparency will help. What do you think? So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok, do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate, and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
